description_long: This is a large, double-edged sword with a straight, tapered blade and a prominent crossguard. The blade appears to be forged from dark metal, giving it a robust and formidable appearance. The crossguard is slightly curved, providing hand protection, and the handle is long and cylindrical, likely designed for two-handed use. The pommel is spherical, adding balance and a classic medieval aesthetic. The entire sword has a matte, slightly textured finish, with no visible engravings or decorative elements. Its size and design suggest it is modeled after a European longsword, suitable for display or use in historical reenactments.
description: A large, double-edged medieval-style sword with a straight blade, curved crossguard, and spherical pommel, all in a dark matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, curved crossguard, and spherical pommel.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the blade and handle, with the crossguard and pommel visible in silhouette.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and pommel from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again highlighting the slimness of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: long, cylindrical, dark, with a spherical pommel
